title: Keel Lorenz Tangs Differently
wikitext:
[]: Keel Lorenz Tangs Differently? Post by swamp147 » Mon Aug 25, 2008 8:20 pm As we see in EoE, when people are tanged during Pre-Instrumentality, they make a splashing sound; sorta like a water balloon popping. However, when Keel tangs, he makes more of a melting sound. I've notices the same thing not only with the sound of the tang, but also the 'way' they tang, When most people tang, it's as if they're a water balloon that just popped (except LCL of course). Keel doesn't pop/splash; again, he sorta melts. Anyone have any ideas or theories on why this is so?

[]: Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Aug 25, 2008 8:26 pm Cause he's a cyborg =P I don't know, that's my input, lol.

[]: Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 25, 2008 8:28 pm Maybe because he wants this to happen? No resistance to the AATF = melting as apposed to popping.

[]: Post by Rhyno » Mon Aug 25, 2008 8:30 pm I'm leaning towards dramatic effect. I don't necessarily see an 'in universe' explanation apparent for his variant tanged effect. Isn't he also the only member of Seele who we see get tanged?

[]: Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 25, 2008 8:32 pm All the others had their monoliths up. When they tang, all we see is the hologram vanish.

[]: Post by Rhyno » Mon Aug 25, 2008 8:38 pm Formless One wrote: Maybe because he wants this to happen? No resistance to the AATF = melting as apposed to popping.

[]: Post by Monk Ed » Mon Aug 25, 2008 8:51 pm I noticed how Keel tanged differently, but I didn't really pay it much mind. There's a lot of randomness that seems to be involved in tanging. For example, Maya's hand flies off from her body and doesn't tang until it impacts something. Also, there's an inconsistency in the order of what parts of the body tang when: Our first view of tanging is a severed arm in a pool of blood. As the Rei apparition manifests over it, the blood turns to LCL, but the arm does not. Then we see a shot of a corridor of empty clothes and tang. The closest set of clothes includes a pool of tang mixing with a pool of blood. So in two consecutive shots, we see blood tanging before a body part, then bodies that have tanged before some blood does. Maybe because he wants this to happen? No resistance to the AATF = melting as apposed to popping.

[]: Post by swamp147 » Mon Aug 25, 2008 8:57 pm Did Fuyutsuki and Maya 'want' to get tanged? They both accepted it happily when it inevitably came, but they still 'popped' instead of melted. But their circumstances were different than Keel's. Keel wanted Instrumentality all along, while Maya didn't, and only experienced a sort of false happiness when comforted by a Rei spectre in the form of Ritsuko. I think Fuyutsuki didn't want Instrumentality either, but realized it was inevitable, so he went along with it. So I guess Keel truly wanted Instrumentality, which is why he tanged differently. Something just came into mind, based on the above. Popping or splashing tends to be correlated more with resistance, like how the rubber of a water balloon keeps the balloon from popping until sufficient pressure is applied. For Keel, though, if he offered no resistance, then he would've just melted, as if the rubber of a water balloon simply disappeared or melted.

[]: Post by Monk Ed » Mon Aug 25, 2008 9:08 pm swamp147 wrote: So I guess Keel truly wanted Instrumentality, which is why he tanged differently.

[]: Post by Rhyno » Mon Aug 25, 2008 9:44 pm I guess I'm back to dramatic effect.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 25, 2008 10:14 pm He's a cyborg lolololololol.

[]: Post by simon » Tue Aug 26, 2008 3:53 am It's because he was praying at the time and praying is a level 4 light magic spell that slows tanging process by 50% and increases chances to avoid splash hits by 30%. Next subject please.

[]: Post by The Imperialist » Tue Aug 26, 2008 9:08 am a) Tanging, may have been done by himself, without the banshee Rei (Plural) coming for him b) He is a cyborg. Ergo, he does turn into LCL, but it had to slide off all his contraptions in his body, that is why he didn't 'pop' and just splash like everybody else. THe important thing is that it had to slide off all of his mechanical parts. Everybody else just turned into a blob and just went straight to the ground, with only their clothes to really bar the laws of gravity. However, a) is the least likely albeit he may have some way of doing it, but I doubt it.

[]: Post by Iron Rooster » Tue Aug 26, 2008 10:35 am I'd have to give my vote for "he's a cyborg". I don't think there's anyone else in the series who has a lot of mechanical body parts.

[]: Post by The Imperialist » Wed Aug 27, 2008 5:11 am Well, obviously, it has to be. I am not too sure why everyoe is having a in depth discussion on how he tangs. I mean, it has to slide off, so obviously, it would not be the same like everybody else, who just turns for a brief moment, a complete blob of LCL floating in air, then crashing to the ground, while Keel, being made of mechanical parts, means that a lot of LCL has to slide off, unlike the more 'normal' people.

[]: Post by The Bastard King » Wed Aug 27, 2008 10:19 am Keel didn't tang. He melted due to extreme temperatures from nearby nuclear power plants going haywire due to lack of supervision because all of the workers at the plant were tanged. Of course, he shut off his pain sensors, being a cyborg and all, so he didn't feel anything.

[]: Post by The Imperialist » Wed Aug 27, 2008 12:40 pm WHAT? Are you trying to be funny, because I really do not have a sense of humouor. Especially somthing as crude as that. Actually, I take that back. I really don't have a sense of humour.

[]: Post by The Bastard King » Wed Aug 27, 2008 1:57 pm Fine. It was due to an influx of spiral power that was generated between Keel's artificial S2 organ (hence explaining his longevity in life) and the occurrence of Third Impact, which was siphoning the spiral energy from everything on the planet into GNR, hence tanging them. The siphoning from the S2 organ slowed the process down with relation to Keel, due to the much larger production of spiral energy from the organ then from the human body, so the tangification of Keel's body correlated directly with the loss of spiral energy within the S2 organ. Hence, the slower process.

[]: Post by simon » Wed Aug 27, 2008 2:07 pm he tangs differently because LELOUCH COMMANDS IT! I know, I know....

[]: Post by Ornette » Wed Aug 27, 2008 3:09 pm Well, so much for this thread. And Imperialist, you're an idiot.

However, when Keel tangs, he makes more of a melting sound. I've notices the same thing not only with the sound of the tang, but also the 'way' they tang, When most people tang, it's as if they're a water balloon that just popped (except LCL of course).
